content.problemStatement: Given the head of a singly linked list containing integers, shift the elements of the linked list to the right by k places and return the head of the modified list. Do not change the values of the nodes, only change the links between nodes.
content.inputFormat: The input will contain the head of a singly linked list and an integer k. The head will be represented as a list of integers, and k will be a single integer.
content.outputFormat: The output will be the head of the modified linked list after rotating it to the right by k places.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a two-pointer approach to find the new tail of the rotated list and then update the head and tail pointers accordingly.

code.Python3:
# Definition for singly-linked list node
class ListNode:
    def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
        self.val = val
        self.next = next

class Solution:
    def rotateRight(self, head: ListNode, k: int) -> ListNode:
        if not head or not head.next or k == 0:
            return head
        curr = head
        length = 1
        while curr.next:
            curr = curr.next
            length += 1
        curr.next = head
        k = k % length
        new_length = length - k
        new_tail = head
        for _ in range(new_length - 1):
            new_tail = new_tail.next
        new_head = new_tail.next
        new_tail.next = None
        return new_head

# Driver code
def printList(head):
    while head:
        print(head.val, end=" ")
        head = head.next

# Create linked list: 1->2->3->4->5
head = ListNode(1)
head.next = ListNode(2)
head.next.next = ListNode(3)
head.next.next.next = ListNode(4)
head.next.next.next.next = ListNode(5)

sol = Solution()
k = 2
newHead = sol.rotateRight(head, k)
printList(newHead)
